name: proto3: implicit presence and camelCase names
info:
This flow encodes and decodes a `TestType` message defined with `syntax = "proto3"`.

proto3 features shown in `examples/protos/proto3.proto`:

- The field `no_more_snake_case` appears as `noMoreSnakeCase` in JavaScript objects because protobuf.js converts snake_case to camelCase by default. Enable "Keep case" on the protobuf-file config node to keep snake_case names.

> [!WARNING]
> **false vs not set:** plain proto3 scalar fields have *implicit presence*. A value equal to the default (`false`, `0`, `""`) is never transmitted and is **missing after decode**. Try it - change `noMoreSnakeCase` to `false` in the inject node and the decoded object will not contain the field at all. "Set to false" and "never set" produce identical bytes.
>
> When your flow must tell them apart, declare the field `optional` in the schema (`optional bool no_more_snake_case = 5;`) or use a proto2 or Editions schema - those keep explicitly set values. See https://protobuf.dev/programming-guides/field_presence/

The protobuf-file config node points at:

    node_modules/node-red-contrib-protobuf/examples/protos/proto3.proto

This relative path works when Node-RED runs from your user directory (for example `~/.node-red`). If the proto file fails to load, open the protobuf-file config node and enter the absolute path on your machine.
